channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Ambitious Chinese GPU vendor debuts liquid-cooled, single-slot, dual GPU card with 48GB RAM and the ability to get four of them in a system - sadly, it's the Intel Arc Pro B60
item.description: MaxSun’s Arc Pro B60 pairs dual GPUs with liquid cooling in a single-slot form, promising dense AI and rendering power.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft just got its hands on 100,000 Nvidia GB300 chips - and all it took was investing $33 billion in these startups
item.description: Microsoft’s $33 billion investment in neocloud providers secures Nvidia GPUs, expands compute access, and raises new antitrust and energy concerns.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: This PC is truly unique - Minisforum M1-1295 is the first mini PC with no front-facing ports, and the audiophile in me will more than appreciate the three separate audio connectors
item.description: Minisforum M1-1295 combines minimalist design and Intel power, hiding all ports at the back for a cleaner workstation aesthetic.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan invite families to join their push against predatory social media practices
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prince Harry and Meghan urged parents to stand against social media companies that they said lure children with predatory algorithms designed to exploit their data amid the “explosion of unregulated artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Google hit by tougher scrutiny from UK’s antitrust watchdog
item.description: Alphabet Inc.'s Google became the first company to be designated with so called strategic market status in the UK, exposing the US firm's online search and advertising business to a closer scrutiny by the country's antitrust watchdog.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: YouTube launches 'second chance' program for banned users
item.description: YouTube is rolling out a "second chance" program to allow previously banned creators to apply for new channels, the company announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple doubles security bounty at Hexagon 2025
item.description: If you know your way around platform security, you don’t need to sell your discoveries to dodgy surveillance-as-a-service firms — Apple will make you a millionaire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to watch South Korea vs Brazil for *FREE*
item.description: Here's our quick guide on how to watch South Korea vs Brazil with both teams already qualified for the 2026 World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: Lessons from the Salesforce breach
item.description: The chilling reality of a Salesforce.com data breach is a jarring wake-up call, not just for its customers, but for the entire cloud computing industry. In recent months, a wave of cyberattacks has targeted cloud-based platforms that house and process massive amounts of personal and corporate data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Taco Bell, KFC franchisee using AI to improve customer experience
item.description: Rather than displacing employees, as some fear AI might, Coach AI can serve as "a tool to help the worker.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft scraps feature that let drivers see how often riders tip
item.description: Lyft has scrapped a test feature that showed some drivers how often passengers tip before accepting a ride.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Too many junk calls to your iPhone? Try Apple’s new iOS 26 call screen feature
item.description: iPhone users have a new tool to combat the scourge of nuisance phone calls: a virtual gatekeeper that can screen incoming calls from unknown numbers. It’s among the bevy of new features that Apple rolled out with last month’s release of iOS 26.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Finance industry encouraged to adopt blockchain by UK government
item.description: The UK government has announced plans to appoint a “digital markets champion” as part of its drive to modernize the way financial transactions are conducted by adopting blockchain-based shared ledgers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: HP's new portable monitor features the world’s first Neo:LED display - here’s why it matters to creative professionals
item.description: HP’s Series 5 Pro 14" introduces Neo:LED tech in a lightweight, color-accurate portable display built for travel professionals.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Amazon sets release date for Melania Trump documentary
item.description: A high-profile documentary on first lady Melania Trump now has a release date, with Amazon MGM Studios saying it'll premiere in theaters in January. "Melania" will be released on Jan. 30 in U.S. theaters before streaming on Prime Video, the media giant announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: HP has an 'affordable' 5K curved ultrawide 49-inch monitor with a 5-megapixel pop-up webcam - it's just a shame about that VA panel
item.description: HP’s 49-inch curved 5K ultrawide monitor targets professionals needing workspace, conferencing tools, and trading flexibility in one affordable setup.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Google and Figma partner in bid to remake real-time design with Gemini 2.5
item.description: Creativity has always been governed by time—not just how long it takes to bring an idea to life, but how long a creator can stay “in flow.” Every designer knows the frustration of an idea hanging in digital limbo. But those pauses, once accepted as inevitable, are now starting to vanish.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: One of the best budget hi-fi makers just launched an irresistible Bluetooth turntable, and there's only one color you should buy it in
item.description: Kanto Audio's first turntable is a serious rival to entry-level Audio-Technica, Pro-ject and Sony spinners
item.dc:creator: Carrie Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Major Discord hack exposes the real risks of digital ID
item.description: The Discord user data breach offers yet another argument against the UK government’s authoritarian plans for Digital ID. A sensible government would consider the implications before forcing people to risk information with a stunt like this.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Leveraging AI-powered defense to secure the NOC at Black Hat USA
item.description: The Black Hat USA Network Operations Center (NOC) is a unique environment, serving as a real-time proving ground for cybersecurity technologies tested by some of the world’s most talented cyber professionals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Cisco’s Michael Overstreet on securing IT-OT interfaces in federal networks
item.description: Agencies face rising cybersecurity risks as IT and OT converge. Cisco security solutions engineer shares strategies for visibility, segmentation and AI.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: CISA’s Matthew Rogers, INL’s Ollie Gagnon on driving cyber resilience in critical infrastructure
item.description: CISA and INL aim to scale free OT cybersecurity services to protect critical infrastructure, as even small orgs need resilience against rising cyber threats.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: UC Santa Barbara develops new soft robotic system for emergency intubation procedures
item.description: UC Santa Barbara researchers developed a soft robotic intubation system that achieved 100% success rates for experts and 96% for paramedics with minimal training.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple’s DMA troubles in Europe continue, but a solution may be in sight
item.description: While the struggle continues and Apple is urging the EU to repeal the legislation, the company may be inching toward some type of settlement with the EU over alleged violations of the bloc’s Digital Markets Act, according to The Financial Times. It’s not a happy talk.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Greenland's leader hails EU as trusted friend and urges investment in its minerals
item.description: Greenland's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen has thanked the European Union for being a "trusted friend" and urged investment in Greenland's resources.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google enhances debugging, performance in Opal, its low-code AI-based app builder
item.description: Google has rolled out new upgrades to Opal, its low-code AI-based application builder, aimed at significantly improving debugging capabilities and its overall performance, as part of its push to streamline AI app development for non-experts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: PyCrucible: An easy way to redistribute your Python apps
item.description: One of Python’s most persistent limitations is how unnecessarily difficult it is to take a Python program and give it to another user as a self-contained click-to-run package. The design of the Python language makes this surprisingly difficult to do.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How GitHub won software development
item.description: In the late 2000s, I was working at Borland, the formerly high-flying software development tools company. My boss and I would regularly sit around tossing out ideas for new products. The Internet was really hitting its stride, and we saw numerous opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JetBrains Amper build tool adds Compose Hot Reload
item.description: JetBrains has released Amper 0.8.0, an update to the build tool for Java and Kotlin that adds support for Compose Hot Reload. Amper 0.8.0 also adds support for custom Kotlin compiler versions and for JavaScript and WebAssembly (Wasm) targets.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tesla offers cheaper versions of 2 electric vehicles in bid to win back market share in tough year
item.description: Tesla rolled out new, cheaper versions of two of its electric car models on Tuesday in hopes the offerings will help revive flagging sales but investors dumped its stock anyway.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 big takeaways from The Hill’s Health Next Summit
item.description: The state of the country’s health care landscape is in flux, according to top health experts and lawmakers, as the federal government shutdown hinges on the battle over health care access and new priorities crystallize under the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Kentucky lawsuit says Roblox fails to protect children on its popular online gaming platform
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky's attorney general claimed Tuesday that the online gaming platform Roblox has become a “playground for predators” as he announced a lawsuit accusing the company of lax child safety measures.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IBM touts agentic AI orchestration, cryptographic risk controls
item.description: At its TechXchange 2025 conference this week, IBM took the wraps off a number of software packages that have been updated to help enterprises more efficiently operate their growing AI infrastructures.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US foreign adversaries use ChatGPT with other AI models in cyber operations: Report
item.description: Malicious actors from U.S. foreign adversaries used ChatGPT jointly with other AI models to conduct various cyber operations, according to a new OpenAI report.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI tech helps a senior reunite with her cat after 11 days
item.description: A woman from Washington reunited with her missing Maine Coon cat Louie after 11 days using Love Lost, a free AI-powered pet recovery platform.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deloitte to partially refund Australian government for report with apparent AI-generated errors
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Deloitte Australia will partially refund the 440,000 Australian dollars ($290,000) paid by the Australian government for a report that was littered with apparent AI-generated errors, including a fabricated quote from a federal court judgment and references to nonexistent
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google's Play Store shake-up looms after Supreme Court refuses to delay overhaul of the monopoly
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday refused to protect Google from a year-old order requiring a major makeover of its Android app store that's designed to unleash more competition against a system that a jury declared an illegal monopoly.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4
